mission.title: cilium: Communication does not work on EKS with AWS VPC CNI and Cilium deployed in chaining mode for the pods running on the same node when SecuirtyGroups For Pods is activated and applied to one of the communicating pods
mission.description: Communication does not work on EKS with AWS VPC CNI and Cilium deployed in chaining mode for the pods running on the same node when SecuirtyGroups For Pods is activated and applied to one of the communicating pods. This issue affects 28+ users.

steps.title: Identify cilium troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cilium deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Communication does not work on EKS with AWS VPC CNI and Cilium deployed in chaining mode for the pods running on the same node when SecuirtyGroups For Pods is activated and applied to one of the communicating pods

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
#### Scenario:
- Pods are running on the same EKS worker node.
- Client application  is running as K8s pod with Security Group For Pods  (SGFP) attached based on the SecurityGroupPolicy definition.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Communication does not work on EKS with AWS VPC CNI and
steps.description:
AWS uses VLAN interfaces for SG routing, and intentionally avoids upserting a route
so that traffic flows through the VPC and SG rules can be evaluated. Cilium will
upsert the route when an endpoint is created, and this causes traffic to be dropped
due to assymetric routing.

This exposes a wider
```yaml
# for init  containers
DISABLE_TCP_EARLY_DEMUX=true

#for main container
ENABLE_POD_ENI = true
POD_SECURITY_GROUP_ENFORCING_MODE=standard
AWS_VPC_K8S_CNI_EXTERNALSNAT=true
ENABLE_PREFIX_DELEGATION=false
```

steps.title: Confirm Communication does not work on EKS with AWS VPC is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
AWS uses VLAN interfaces for SG routing, and intentionally avoids upserting a route
so that traffic flows through the VPC and SG rules can be evaluated. Cilium will
upsert the route when an endpoint is created, and this causes traffic to be dropped
due to assymetric routing.
codeSnippets:
# for init  containers
DISABLE_TCP_EARLY_DEMUX=true

#for main container
ENABLE_POD_ENI = true
POD_SECURITY_GROUP_ENFORCING_MODE=standard
AWS_VPC_K8S_CNI_EXTERNALSNAT=true
ENABLE_PREFIX_DELEGATION=false

cni:
  chainingMode: aws-cni
  exclusive: false

enableIPv4Masquerade: false
tunnel: disabled
		
endpointRoutes:
  enabled: true

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
